top_signals.title: Borrowing costs jump after Burnham ‘fiscal flexibility’ remarks
top_signals.summary: The government’s borrowing costs climbed sharply on Monday afternoon after Andy Burnham said he planned to juice government borrowing by taking advantage of “flexibility” in the current fiscal rules. The new Prime Minister told reporters that his administration would use all potential room within the self-imposed spending straitjacket, which he had promised not to loosen [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Top China and Philippines envoys to meet for first time since 2024
top_signals.summary: Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi is scheduled to meet with Philippine counterpart Theresa Lazaro on the sidelines of the ASEAN foreign ministers meeting in Manila.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: Personal Income and Outlays, April 2026
top_signals.summary: Personal income decreased less than $0.1 billion (less than 0.1 percent at a monthly rate) in April, according to estimates released today by the U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis (BEA). Disposable personal income (DPI)-personal income less personal current taxes-decreased $19.9 billion (0.1 percent), and personal consumption expenditures (PCE) increased $111.1 billion (0.5 percent). Personal outlays-the sum of PCE, personal interest payments, and personal current transfer payments-increased $114.0 billion in April. Personal saving was $611.7 billion in April, and the personal saving rate-personal saving as a percentage of DPI-was 2.6 percent. Full Text
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: Romesh Ranganathan makes it hard to defend the BBC
top_signals.summary: There is a lot to love about the BBC. But the new Romesh Ranganathan-fronted Corporation love-in will backfire, writes Matt Kenyon I’ve been getting sentimental about the BBC lately. Two different people have separately reminded me of Merlin; I had my first encounter with Louis Theroux’s Neil and Christine Hamilton documentary; I’ve been strangely hypnotised [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: One year on, Trump tariffs continue battering Japan automakers
top_signals.summary: In fiscal 2025, which ended in March this year, combined costs from the Trump tariffs at six major Japanese automakers, including industry leader Toyota, exceeded ¥2.4 trillion.
